mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines by more than 25 basis points over a 3-month window, the reduction in short-term borrowing costs triggers a compression of equity risk premiums and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.2% as of Nov 2018 reflects recent Fed rate cuts
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines > 0.25pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum and consumer income growth, which translates into increased corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 149,893 thousand as of Nov 2018 near historical highs
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5%) during periods of sustained PAYEMS growth > 150k monthly additions, or Sharpe ratio of the signal < 0.3 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,200 thousand units signals weakening residential investment demand and forward-looking economic contraction risk, which causes equity investors to reduce risk exposure and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as sentiment shifts.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,200 thousand units in current month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,228 thousand as of Oct 2018, near trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with leading indicator deterioration
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances where HOUST < 1,200k, or the signal produces a Sharpe ratio < 0.2 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, indicating slowing manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side weakness that constrains profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth rate falls below 0.5%
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances where INDPRO MoM growth < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates AND the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously by more than 0.2 percentage points over 2 months, the joint signal indicates consumer demand weakness combined with labor market deterioration, triggering a negative equity market response over 20 trading days as recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth < 0.3% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 513,531 million and UNRATE at 3.7% as of Nov 2018
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging recessionary signals
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of instances where both conditions are met, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% year-over-year AND the federal funds rate remains below the inflation rate, real rates are negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations through lower real discount rates, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL YoY rate
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with moderate inflation
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of instances where both conditions hold, or the interaction effect is not significant (p > 0.10) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.0% after a sustained period below 4.0%, signaling a shift from tight labor market conditions to loosening, which reduces wage pressure on corporate profit margins and produces positive equity returns over 20 trading days as margin expansion expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 4.0% after 6+ months below 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% as of Nov 2018, below 4.0% threshold
regime_hint: transition from full employment to labor market normalization
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the signal produces a Sharpe ratio < 0.25 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, indicating late-cycle slowdown and reduced earnings growth expectations, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2.0% in latest quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18,671.65 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q3 2018
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
